governing_requirement: Persist a trusted-signed, checkpointed, append-only bridge journal and closed durable state machine with atomic idempotency, exact restart reconstruction, unknown-submission reconciliation before retry, and deterministic detection of collision, illegal transition, fork, deletion, reorder, stale head, and interruption at every durable boundary.
prerequisites: MF-P6-10.06 has no accepted invalidation publication ledger.
MF-P6-11.01 has no accepted external adapter/conformance boundary.
No producer/consumer journal head has been mutually adopted for a production release.
verified_foundations: The frozen strict lifecycle event schema and authoritative transition matrix cover release/adoption, capability/requirements, request submission/admission/queue/run/unknown/reconciliation/completion, receipt, retry, circuit, cache, certificate, repair, recovery, and rollback events with fixed producer roles.

The in-memory chain validator checks document shape, canonical payload hash, trusted role-specific Ed25519 signature, production key state, unique event IDs, contiguous sequence, stream/epoch, prior hash, causation, monotonic time, legal producer/state transitions, replayed resource state, reconciliation evidence, invalidation binding, expected head, and checkpoint range/head fields.
The generic batch validator accepts same-key/same-body replay and rejects same-key/different-body plus changed-payload nonce replay.
Focused lifecycle, four reconciliation outcomes, idempotency/error, and atomic append-once tests pass; Ruff passes.

Existing workflow StateDB demonstrates SQLite WAL plus a single-writer guard, and failure mining demonstrates locked append, flush, fsync, and exact-identity append-once behavior. These are reusable mechanics, not bridge authority.

commands: python -m ruff check src/maskfactory/validation.py src/maskfactory/state.py src/maskfactory/qa/failure_mining.py tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py tests/test_failure_mining_coverage.py
in-memory signed two-event chain, checkpoint, deletion/reorder, and request idempotency probes

direct_probes.gap: The caller-provided checkpoint is compared with the chain but is not itself authenticated or loaded from a trusted durable store.

direct_probes.interpretation: In-memory deletion is detected when the expected chain/checkpoint is supplied.
The pure batch validator has correct basic collision semantics but persists no result for replay.

direct_probes.gap: Validation reports issues only; it does not return or persist the authoritative resource/request/cache/adoption/recovery state.

confirmed_gaps: No bridge journal database/JSONL store, append command, replay command, checkpoint publisher, state snapshot, or recovery entry point exists.
No atomic transaction binds event append, idempotency reservation/result, materialized resource state, output/receipt, and signed checkpoint/head with receipt-last semantics.
The checkpoint accepted by validate_bridge_event_chain is an unsigned mapping; standalone authenticity, signer role, revocation, stream ownership, and persisted-head identity are not established.
The in-memory validator always expects sequence to start at one and cannot validate/replay an authenticated retained suffix from a prior signed checkpoint without the complete history.
No durable idempotency ledger returns the prior result for same-key/same-body or prevents concurrent same-key/different-body execution before either writer commits.
No materialized closed state is reconstructed across request, route, lease, remote submission, validation, cache/decision, feedback, adoption, invalidation, repair, recovery, and rollback resources.

The frozen event vocabulary has no explicit route-selected, lease-acquired/lost, result-received/validated, decision-recorded, or cancellation events; no additive internal-state mapping or negotiated next contract version closes those boundaries.
No restart reconciler detects submitted/outcome-unknown work, queries exact remote execution identity/history, and authorizes retry only after verified not-found.

No fault injection kills the process at reservation, append, provider submission, result, artifact write, receipt, state materialization, checkpoint, or fsync/rename boundaries and proves exact recovery/no duplicate authority.

Existing workflow SQLite and failure-mining JSONL stores do not carry bridge signatures, wire events, cross-project heads, idempotency bodies/results, or bridge state and receive mechanics-only credit.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement an additive producer durable journal/state engine over frozen signed wire events, with authenticated checkpoints, atomic idempotency/results, exact replay/recovery, and an explicit internal-state-to-wire mapping without mutating frozen v1 contracts.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. Candidate paths are read-only. The scope is disjoint from frozen v1 contracts, preserved MF-P6-08.05 dirty paths, and prepared MF-P6-10.06/11.01-11.05 paths.
validator_commands: python -m pytest -q tests/test_bridge_durable_journal.py tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py -k 'event or journal or idempotency or reconciliation or recovery'
python -m ruff check src/maskfactory/bridge/durable_journal.py tools/replay_bridge_journal.py tests/test_bridge_durable_journal.py
acceptance_criteria: Use a contained single-writer transactional store with durable event bytes, canonical body hash, trusted signature verification result, idempotency reservation/result, materialized state, evidence references, and checkpoint/head tables; enable WAL/synchronous durability and detect corruption.

Append only an exact trusted valid next event whose stream/epoch/sequence/prior hash/causation/producer/transition match the committed head and materialized state; concurrent stale-head/fork writers fail without mutation.

Atomically reserve idempotency before external execution, return the exact prior receipt for same-key/same-body, reject same-key/different-body, and represent submitted-but-outcome-unknown across crashes.

Persist and authenticate checkpoint evidence binding genesis/range/head/event count/materialized-state hash/idempotency head/trust and revocation heads; unsigned caller mappings cannot establish a trusted restart point.

Replay from genesis or an independently verified checkpoint plus retained suffix produces the exact same closed state and head; missing/injected/reordered/forked events, changed bytes, stale trust, and checkpoint drift fail closed.

Model every required durable boundary across admit, route, lease, submit known/unknown, reconcile, result, validate, cache/decision, feedback, adoption, invalidation, retry/repair, cancellation, recovery, and rollback. Map internal states explicitly to frozen v1 events and require a negotiated new wire version for semantics v1 cannot represent rather than overloading fields.

On restart, reconcile every incomplete transaction from journal/receipt/remote identity; never infer success from files and never resubmit unknown remote work until exact not-found evidence authorizes it.

Commit artifacts/output first, then signed receipt/event/state, and checkpoint last under a documented recoverable transaction; no state may claim authority before exact receipt/certificate bytes exist.

Kill/fault tests at every reservation, append, fsync, provider submit, result, artifact, receipt, state, and checkpoint boundary prove no duplicate execution/authority, no orphan promotion, immutable history, and deterministic terminal recovery.
Frozen v1 contracts, preserved dirty paths, other stores, and unrelated runtime/worker state remain byte-identical.

claim_boundary: This audit credits the signed in-memory event/transition/checkpoint comparison validator, idempotency collision semantics, reconciliation evidence, and reusable durability primitives only. It does not persist a bridge journal, authenticate a checkpoint, reconstruct state after restart, reconcile live remote work, pass crash drills, or complete MF-P6-11.06.
